issues.problem: Several frontend list APIs send only pagination and local filters, without automatically adding the current systemId and tenantId. Some pages also request data while appId or moduleId is still empty. The matching backend GET endpoints in ConfigController, RuntimeRecordController, WorkflowController, FileController, and OpenApiManageController declare systemId, tenantId, appId, or moduleId as required @RequestParam values. As a result, app config, record list, workflow template, file list, and OpenAPI client pages can fail on first load or after only entering system context, breaking the frontend-backend business loop.
issues.suggestion: Add a shared frontend enrichQuery/enrichPayload path for GET queries so systemId and tenantId are always included, and block requests that truly require appId or moduleId until those values are selected. On the backend, either fill context from the token or mark real filters as required=false so docs, frontend calls, and controller parameters are consistent.

issues.problem: Platform center operations lack platform-level permission checks. createTenant, createSystem, and updateSystemStatus do not verify that the current user has platform administration permission. enterSystem only loads an enabled system by systemId, ignores the requested tenantId, and does not verify that the current account is a system member, owner, or authorized administrator. Any authenticated account can enter any enabled system context and receive a token containing that systemId and tenantId.
issues.suggestion: Add platform role or permission checks for tenant and system creation and status changes. enterSystem must verify that request tenantId matches the system tenant and that the current account is a system member, owner, or authorized role; otherwise return FORBIDDEN.

issues.problem: OpenAPI GET detail authenticates HMAC and scope, then directly calls runtimeRecordManageService.detail(recordId) without setting CurrentUser. Since /api/v1/open/** is anonymous in SecurityConfig, the internal PermissionService/SecurityContext path returns unauthorized, so the open record detail API is not usable. POST create sets CurrentUser.account to admin to bypass PermissionService and checks only MODULE/ACTION scope, without verifying that systemId, tenantId, and appId match the OpenAPI client ownership or granted scope. This allows cross-system or cross-tenant writes if a caller submits those IDs.
issues.suggestion: Do not impersonate the platform admin for OpenAPI calls. Create a dedicated OpenAPI security context after authentication, validate client ownership and scope for systemId, tenantId, appId, moduleId, and action, and make GET and POST use either a shared internal-recognized context or dedicated service methods.

issues.problem: Workflow task listing is not filtered by current user, systemId, tenantId, assignee, or candidate scope, and does not call PermissionService. handleTask only checks the workflow:handle action and does not verify that the current user is the task assignee or candidate. A user with the action permission can view or process tasks from other systems or other approvers, which violates the PRD task permission model.
issues.suggestion: Filter task lists by token context, assignee/candidate, and data scope. When handling a task, validate task ownership, candidate or assignee identity, transfer target, admin override rules, task status, and systemId/tenantId boundaries.

issues.problem: Record create and update only validate required fields. They do not return field-level errors before type conversion, do not validate enum, dictionary, department, member, or related-record values and permissions, and do not proactively check unique-field conflicts before saving. Number and date conversion errors fall through as generic parameter or system errors, so the PRD requirements for field validation, uniqueness validation, related-data permission checks, and clear errors are not met.
issues.suggestion: Validate values based on ModuleField.fieldType and validateRule, including enum/dictionary/member/department/related-record existence and permissions. Before saving unique fields, check by systemId, tenantId, moduleId, fieldId, and valueHash while excluding the current record, then return CONFLICT or a field-level error.

issues.problem: The build report and parts of the frontend/backend source text display mojibake, and the report states that the configured JDK, Maven, and Node paths are unavailable while the build succeeded only through equivalent tools found on PATH. The PRD explicitly lists document, page, or configuration encoding problems as acceptance exceptions. This creates both encoding acceptance risk and build reproduction risk.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite the build report and source UI text as UTF-8 or ASCII-safe content, and fix the configured tool paths or document the actual JDK, Maven, and Node versions and commands used so the build can be reproduced without relying on incidental PATH entries.
